title: Verify and Control Limit Connections to and Use of External Systems
description: verify and control/limit connections to and use of external systems

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of connections to external systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern documented in network diagrams and the SSP and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of external system use is an organizational GRC documentation concern documented in acceptable use policies and the SSP and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Verified
checks.purpose: Verify that outbound connections to external systems are authenticated via certificate or proxy and that Windows Defender Firewall outbound rules restrict unapproved external destinations

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Verified
checks.purpose: Verify that external system use is verified via proxy authentication or VPN split tunneling controls and that audit logging captures outbound connection activity

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Controlled and Limited
checks.purpose: Verify that outbound connections are restricted via Windows Defender Firewall outbound rules, proxy settings enforce approved destinations, and direct internet access is blocked for sensitive ports

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Controlled and Limited
checks.purpose: Verify that removable media from external systems is restricted, cloud storage sync clients are controlled via GP, and external system access requires VPN or proxy authentication

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of connections to external systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of external system use is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Verified
checks.purpose: Verify that outbound server connections to external systems are restricted to approved destinations via Windows Defender Firewall outbound rules and IPSec authentication

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Verified
checks.purpose: Verify that external system use from the server is verified via proxy or firewall authentication logging and that outbound connection events are captured in audit logs

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Controlled and Limited
checks.purpose: Verify that outbound connections from the server are restricted to approved external destinations via Windows Defender Firewall outbound block rules and that default outbound policy is block

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Controlled and Limited
checks.purpose: Verify that server access to external cloud services is restricted via GP, removable media from external systems is blocked, and IPSec enforces authenticated connections to approved external systems

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of connections to external systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of external system use is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Verified
checks.purpose: Verify that outbound connections to external systems are authenticated via SSH keys or certificates and that firewall rules restrict unapproved external destinations

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Verified
checks.purpose: Verify that external system use is logged via auditd outbound connection rules and that proxy or firewall authentication captures external access activity

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Controlled and Limited
checks.purpose: Verify that outbound connections are restricted via iptables nftables or firewalld OUTPUT chain rules and that default outbound policy blocks unapproved external destinations

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Controlled and Limited
checks.purpose: Verify that external system access requires proxy authentication or VPN, removable media from external systems is restricted via udev rules, and SELinux or AppArmor limits external network access

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of connections to external systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of external system use is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Verified
checks.purpose: Verify that outbound connections to external systems are authenticated via SSH keys or certificates and that firewall rules restrict unapproved external destinations

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Verified
checks.purpose: Verify that external system use is logged via auditd outbound connection rules and that proxy or firewall authentication captures external access activity

checks.name: Connections to External Systems are Controlled and Limited
checks.purpose: Verify that outbound connections are restricted via iptables nftables or firewalld OUTPUT chain rules and that default outbound policy blocks unapproved external destinations

checks.name: Use of External Systems is Controlled and Limited
checks.purpose: Verify that external system access requires proxy authentication or VPN, removable media from external systems is restricted via udev rules, and AppArmor limits external network access

remediation: Document all approved connections to external systems in the System Security Plan and maintain a current inventory. On Windows, configure Windows Defender Firewall outbound rules to block connections to external systems not explicitly authorized and set the default outbound policy to block for sensitive network profiles. Require VPN or proxy authentication for any approved external connections involving CUI. On Linux, configure iptables or nftables OUTPUT chain rules to permit only approved external destinations and block all others by default. Review and recertify all external connections at least annually and execute Interconnection Security Agreements for any connections involving CUI sharing.
